sections.content:
Looking at the image, the bar is now at the top of the corridor again (rows 15-19, cols 34-38). The bar moved UP 14 from rows 45-49 to rows 31-35, but then it seems to have moved further UP to rows 15-19. This is because the corridor is narrow, and the bar moved UP until it hit the wall at the top.
Wait, but the bar was at rows 45-49, cols 19-23. Moving UP 14 should put it at rows 31-35, cols 19-23. But the image shows the bar at the top of the corridor (rows 15-19, cols 34-38). This doesn't match.
Let me check the current bar position.
Actually, looking at the image, the bar is at the top of the corridor, just below the black box. This is the starting position. So the bar has returned to the starting position again.
This is very strange. Every time I move the bar around, it eventually returns to the starting position. This suggests the game has some reset mechanic or the bar is constrained to the starting position.
Wait, maybe the bar is not the player-controlled object. Maybe the player controls something else, and the bar is just a visual element that moves based on some other mechanic.
Let me reconsider the game. The black box at the top has a blue L-shape. The blue box at the bottom-left has a blue L-shape. These might be the player-controlled objects, and the bar is just a visual indicator.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the bar is at the top of the corridor. The white cross is in the main room. The black box at the top has a blue L-shape. The blue box at the bottom-left has a blue L-shape.
Maybe the goal is to move the blue L-shape in the black box to match the blue L-shape in the blue box. Or maybe the goal is to move the bar to the white cross.
I'm confused about what the player controls. Let me check the current bar position and see if there are any other objects that might be player-controlled.
